hero.title: Expose your local MCP servers to the
hero.subtitle: MCP Bridge relays public HTTP requests to the Model Context Protocol servers running on your machine, over a secure outbound WebSocket. No router configuration, no static IP, no inbound firewall rules.
hero.snippetComment: any MCP client can call your local server
hero.snippetArrow: forwarded to your desktop agent, then to localhost

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Four steps between your laptop and a public MCP endpoint.

register.title: Create an account
register.description: Sign up and register the MCPs you want to expose, each with a name and the local port it runs on.

agent.title: Run the desktop agent
agent.description: The agent opens a single outbound WebSocket connection to the bridge — nothing needs to be reachable from the internet.

connect.title: Get a public URL
connect.description: Every MCP gets a stable address: bridge.example.com/mcp/<username>-<mcp-name>.

use.title: Use it anywhere
use.description: Point ChatGPT or any other MCP-capable client at the URL. Requests are relayed to your machine and back in real time.

architecture.title: One relay, zero open ports
architecture.subtitle: The backend never connects to your machine — your agent always dials out to it.

client.title: MCP client
client.description: ChatGPT or any other tool that speaks MCP over HTTP

backend.title: MCP Bridge
backend.description: Routes each request to the right account's connected agent

agent.title: Desktop agent
agent.description: Forwards the request to your local MCP server and sends the response back

architecture.note: Because the connection is always initiated by your desktop agent, there's nothing to expose, forward, or leave open on your network.

features.title: Built for running MCPs from anywhere
features.subtitle: A small, focused feature set instead of a general-purpose tunnel.

noPortForwarding.title: No port forwarding
noPortForwarding.description: Your agent connects outbound only. No router changes, no static IP, no inbound rules.

multipleMcps.title: Multiple MCPs per account
multipleMcps.description: Register as many local MCP servers as you like, each with its own name and public URL.

secureAuth.title: Account-scoped access
secureAuth.description: Every proxied request resolves to a specific MCP owned by a specific, authenticated account.

realtime.title: Real-time relay
realtime.description: Requests are forwarded over a persistent WebSocket connection with correlation IDs and timeouts.

anyClient.title: Works with any MCP client
anyClient.description: Plain HTTP in, plain HTTP out — compatible with ChatGPT and any other MCP-speaking client.

openArchitecture.title: Open, self-hostable architecture
openArchitecture.description: A documented WebSocket protocol and a clean backend you can run and extend yourself.

whatIsMcp.question: What is an MCP server?
whatIsMcp.answer: The Model Context Protocol is a standard way for AI tools to call out to external servers for context and actions. MCP Bridge doesn't run MCP servers for you — it exposes ones you already run locally.

security.question: Is my machine exposed to the internet?
security.answer: No. Your desktop agent only makes an outbound connection to the bridge. The bridge never initiates a connection to your machine or localhost.

selfHost.question: Can I self-host the backend?
selfHost.answer: Yes. MCP Bridge is a standard NestJS backend with an open, versioned WebSocket protocol, designed to be run on your own infrastructure.

multipleAgents.question: Can I run more than one MCP at a time?
multipleAgents.answer: Yes. A single desktop agent connection can serve every MCP configured on your account, each reachable at its own public URL.

finalCta.title: Ready to expose your first MCP?
finalCta.subtitle: Create an account, register your local MCP, and get a public URL in minutes.

footer.tagline: A relay for local MCP servers.
